name: wordpress-router
description: Use when the user asks about WordPress codebases (plugins, themes, block themes, Gutenberg blocks, WP core checkouts) and you need to quickly classify the repo and route to the correct workflow/skill (blocks, theme.json, REST API, WP-CLI, performance, security, testing, release packaging).
capabilities: Classifies WordPress project types including plugin, theme, block theme, WP core, and Gutenberg
Routes tasks to specialized domain skills based on project kind and user intent
Detects available tooling such as Composer, Node, and @wordpress/scripts
Identifies test frameworks present including PHPUnit, Playwright, and wp-env
author: Automattic
securitySummary: Pure documentation-only skill containing markdown files that guide AI routing decisions. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file system access. All 52 static findings are false positives: markdown backticks were misidentified as shell execution, source URLs as network exfiltration, and content hashes as weak cryptography.
valueStatement: Users need help navigating complex WordPress project structures and selecting appropriate development workflows. This skill automatically classifies WordPress codebases and routes tasks to the correct domain-specific skills, ensuring proper handling of plugins, themes, blocks, and core development.
userTitle: Route WordPress tasks to specialized workflows
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files directly
Requires WP-Project-Triage script to be available for automated classification
Cannot route without clear project type detection

useCases.title: Plugin Development Routing
useCases.description: Route plugin development tasks to wp-plugin-development skill for hooks, settings, and REST endpoints

useCases.title: Theme Development Routing
useCases.description: Route theme tasks to wp-block-themes or wp-plugin-development based on classic or block theme approach

useCases.title: WP Core Contribution
useCases.description: Route WordPress core contributions to wp-core workflows with proper PHPUnit and build tooling

promptTemplates.title: Basic Routing
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the wordpress-router skill to classify this WordPress project and tell me which skill to use for my request.

promptTemplates.title: With Project Context
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the WordPress router on this project to identify its type, available tooling, and route my request to the appropriate skill.

promptTemplates.title: Intent-Based Routing
promptTemplates.prompt: Given my WordPress project type and intent to [specific task], use wordpress-router to determine the correct domain skill and approach.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Skill Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Use wordpress-router to analyze my [plugin/theme/core] project and create a workflow plan that may involve multiple specialized skills.

bestPractices: Use this skill at the start of every WordPress task for proper routing
Verify detected versions and tooling before starting implementation
Follow the guardrails checklist before making any changes
antiPatterns: Skipping routing and directly using a domain skill without classification
Assuming project type without running triage detection
Ignoring version constraints that affect development approach

faq.question: Which WordPress project types does this skill support?
faq.answer: Plugins, classic themes, block themes, block plugins, Gutenberg monorepo, WP core, and full site repos.

faq.question: What workflows can this skill route to?
faq.answer: Interactivity API, Abilities API, block development, REST API, WP-CLI, testing, and performance optimization.

faq.question: Is WP-CLI required to use this skill?
faq.answer: Some workflows require WP-CLI but basic routing works without it. The skill detects WP-CLI availability.

faq.question: Does this skill access any files or data?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based routing guide that describes what other skills should read for classification.

faq.question: How does triage detection work?
faq.answer: The skill references an external wp-project-triage script and provides the decision tree for interpreting results.

faq.question: Can this skill be used with other WordPress skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to route to specialized WordPress skills like wp-block-development, wp-plugin-development, and wp-testing.
